sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 257104, "Black Butte River", Stream, CA, Glenn/Mendocino Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":257104,"gaz_name":"Black Butte River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Glenn"/"Mendocino","objectid_1":924461,"isunknowncoords":2}, mouth point (Web Mercator -13676559.4864, 4807205.2938 → 39.59461°N, 122.85862°W). Confirms the file's existing coordinates (-122.8586, 39.5947), which are within ~10 m of the GNIS mouth point.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(18) — Black Butte River and tributaries (Glenn Co.) except Cold Creek
sources.quotedText: (18) Black Butte River and tributaries (Glenn Co.) except Cold Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Applies to the Black Butte River main stem and all its tributaries in Glenn County, except Cold Creek (a tributary), which is not covered by this closure and is separately regulated.

regulations.humanSummary: Black Butte River and its tributaries (except Cold Creek) are closed to all fishing year-round
regulations.verbatimText: Black Butte River and tributaries (Glenn Co.) except Cold Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. This water is closed to all fishing all year, so the license requirement is moot here, but is included for statewide-standard completeness.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
